title: OperateCondition — workflow node/branch condition groups (shared type, workflow module)
description: Condition tree for workflow data nodes and branches: `hap workflow node save-action / save-search / save-get-more --condition`, branch nodes' operateCondition, trigger filters in `node batch-add`. Shape: a 2-D ARRAY — the OUTER array is OR groups, each INNER array is AND-ed items. This is NOT the worksheet FilterCondition type despite similar intent: the field key here is `filedId` (upstream spelling, sic — also filedTypeId/filedValue), the operator is the STRING enum conditionId, values are objects in conditionValues, and grouping is positional instead of spliceType. A workflow node config key named `filters` is ALSO this shape, not the worksheet one.

items.description: One AND group; groups are OR-ed together.

filedId.description: Field (control) id being tested. Spelling is 'filedId' on the wire — 'fieldId' is silently ignored.

conditionId.description: Operator as a STRING enum, e.g. "1"=contains, "2"=equals, "7"=is empty, "8"=is not empty, "9"=>, "10"=>=, "11"=<, "12"=<=, "15"=date is (date fields remap some operators — read an existing node with `hap workflow node get` to copy the exact id). Full table: workflow nodes reference doc.

conditionValues.description: Comparison values as objects: literal = {"value": "x"}; dynamic compare against another node's field = {"value": "", "nodeId": "<nodeId>", "controlId": "<fieldId>"}.

nodeId.description: Which node's record the tested field comes from (the trigger node for trigger filters).

sourceType.description: Source kind of the tested field (1 = worksheet record).
